title: genomics-public-data google cloud bucket permission denied
author: ypradat
body:
Dear GATK team,

Thanks for the great job!

I would like to download some public resources you are using in your pipelines. However, the Google Bucket gs://genomics-public-data listed here https://gatk.broadinstitute.org/hc/en-us/articles/360035890811-Resource-bundle is not accessible publicly.
I get an error message "Additional permissions required to list objects in this bucket. Ask a bucket owner to grant you 'storage.objects.list' permission. Learn more."

Could you please look into this and reset permissions to make it publicly accessible and allow public download?

Many thanks,
Yoann

title: Glimpse Ligate output vcf index not compatible with GATK 4.6.0
author: jsotobroad
body:
We ran into an issue where the output vcf index created by a Glimpse `ligate` command returned an `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` when passing that vcf + index to GATK's `VariantsToTable` command.  bcftools was able to grab regions from the vcf using the same index

When you take that same vcf and run tabix on it to create an index, `VariantsToTable` works without an issue.  

This was discovered while doing some work on the [low pass imputation workflow](https://github.com/broadinstitute/warp/blob/develop/pipelines/wdl/glimpse/low_pass_imputation/Glimpse2LowPassImputation.wdl).

title: ReblockGVCF: "Query asks for data past end of contig" caused by stale position across chromosome boundary (GATK 4.6.2.0)
author: shaze
body:
ReblockGVCF crashes with `htsjdk.samtools.SAMException: Query asks for data past end of contig` when processing a GVCF containing a homozygous ALT SNP near the end of one chromosome followed by a complex MIXED multiallelic variant early on the next chromosome. 

The error occurs in ReblockGVCF.addRefBlockIfNecessary which queries the reference at position 198232730 on chr4, a coordinate that corresponds to the SNP position on chr3 (198232729 + 1) rather than any valid chr4 position. 

This suggests that an internal position variable is not being reset at chromosome boundaries, causing a stale coordinate from chr3 to be used in reference lookups on chr4. The bug is reproducible with a minimal two-record GVCF (one SNP on chr3, one MIXED variant on chr4) against the GRCh38 no-alt analysis set reference.

I attach the example GVCF file. (in the real data there a few dozen variants between the two records and the chr4 record is not the first record in chrom4)

This is the error I get

```
atk ReblockGVCF -R GRCh38_no_alt_analysis_set.fasta  -V reproducer.g.vcf.gz  -O LP2100143-DNA_B09
.vcf.gz  --drop-low-quals -do-qual-approx
Using GATK jar /opt/exp_soft/bioinf/gatk/gatk-4.6.2.0/gatk-package-4.6.2.0-local.jar
Running:

dk.compression_level=2 -jar /opt/exp_soft/bioinf/gatk/gatk-4.6.2.0/gatk-package-4.6.2.0-local.jar ReblockGVCF -R GRCh38_no_alt_analysis_set.fasta -V reproducer.g.vcf.gz -O see.vcf.gz --drop-low-quals -do-qual-approx

title: AnalyzeSaturationMutagenesis does not parse modern CIGAR strings
author: odcambc
body: CIGAR strings in samtools versions >= 1.4 allows "X" and "=" for substitutions and matches, rather than just "M." AnalyzeSaturationMutagenesis does not parse "X" or "=" and requires output in 1.3 or before format.

title: AnalyzeSaturationMutagenesis does not produce HGVS-compliant output
author: odcambc
body:
The output of AnalyzeSaturationMutagenesis has some minor differences with recommended HGVS nomenclature for protein variation.

Current nomenclature guide for proteins here: https://hgvs-nomenclature.org/stable/recommendations/protein/substitution/

* Variants lack the protein indicator: p.(Trp24Cys) vs Trp24Cys. Since the tool analyzes DNA, predicted consequence in parenthesis is appropriate.
* Amino acids are represented as single letters rather than three letters.
* Synonymous mutations are not given a protein consequence value: should be p.(Cys188=)
* Combined insertion/deletions are written "insdel" rather than "delins"
* Duplications are not called
* Single-codon frameshifts without stops in the ORF are not identified as frameshifts
* Extensions are not called. The may be difficult to handle. ASM requires a defined ORF but these by definition involve 5' or 3' elements. Depending on the flanking context provided, these may be impossible to properly handle.

title: GenomicsDBImport completes successfully, but SelectVariants/GenotypeGVCFs hangs at “Starting traversal” when reading gendb:// workspace
author: C-YONG
body:
Dear GATK team,

I am encountering an issue when querying a GenomicsDB workspace generated by GenomicsDBImport.

I rebuilt the workspace from scratch and GenomicsDBImport completed successfully, but downstream tools such as SelectVariants and GenotypeGVCFs appear to hang when reading from the gendb:// workspace.

Here are the details of my setup:

GATK version: 4.6.2.0
Java: OpenJDK 21.0.6
OS: Linux x86_64
GenomicsDB native library version: 1.5.5-addd795
1. GenomicsDBImport command

I used a per-chromosome import with 1250 samples:

gatk --java-options "-Xmx200g -Xms200g -Djava.io.tmpdir=/mnt/k/disease/temp" GenomicsDBImport \
  --genomicsdb-workspace-path /mnt/k/disease/GenomicsDB/chr1 \
  --intervals chr1 \
  --batch-size 60 \
  --sample-name-map /mnt/k/disease/GenomicsDB/chr1.map \
  --reader-threads 10 \
  --tmp-dir /mnt/k/disease/temp

The import completed successfully with the following ending in the log:

Done importing batch 21/21
Import of all batches to GenomicsDB completed!
Shutting down engine
Tool returned:
true
2. Workspace characteristics
Chromosome: chr1
Interval size processed during import: 35,787,290 bp
Number of samples: 1250

The workspace contains expected files such as:

callset.json
vidmap.json
vcfheader.vcf
3. Problematic downstream query

When I try to read the workspace using SelectVariants:

gatk --java-options "-Xmx8g -Djava.io.tmpdir=/mnt/k/tmp" SelectVariants \
  -R /mnt/k/disease/ref/genome.fa \
  -V gendb:///mnt/k/disease/GenomicsDB/chr1 \
  -O /mnt/k/disease/vcf/chr1.vcf.gz

the tool starts normally, but then stops after:

Initializing engine
GenomicsDB native library version : 1.5.5-addd795
No valid combination operation found for INFO field InbreedingCoeff
No valid combination operation found for INFO field MLEAC
No valid combination operation found for INFO field MLEAF

and does not make further progress.

I observed similar behavior previously with both SelectVariants and GenotypeGVCFs, often appearing to stall around or after Starting traversal.

4. Additional notes
The GenomicsDB workspace was freshly rebuilt.
The import completed successfully without reported errors.
System resources do not appear to be exhausted when the query hangs.
I am not sure whether this is due to:
querying the entire chromosome without -L,
a workspace consistency issue,
a problem with some imported gVCFs,
or a compatibility/performance issue with gendb:// querying in this setup.
5. Questions

Could you please advise on the following?

Is this behavior expected when querying a whole-chromosome GenomicsDB workspace with 1250 samples using SelectVariants?
Are there known issues in GATK 4.6.2.0 / GenomicsDB 1.5.5 where a workspace imports successfully but later hangs during traversal?
Are there recommended validation steps to confirm that the workspace is healthy after import?
Would you suggest testing with fewer samples, a smaller interval, or different Java memory settings for downstream querying?

I would appreciate any guidance on how to diagnose whether this is a workspace issue, an input gVCF issue, or expected behavior for a large query.

title: changed BQ permissions - update to documentation needed
author: bheavner
body: Google has updated the BigQuery permissions/roles, so [this section ](https://github.com/broadinstitute/gatk/blob/ah_var_store/scripts/variantstore/beta_docs/gvs-quickstart.md)of the documentation needs to be updated: "Grant your proxy group the BigQuery Data Editor, BigQuery Job User, and BigQuery Read Session User roles on the Google Project that you created in Step 3 (above) that contains your BigQuery dataset. For step-by-step instructions, see the Google Cloud documentation article, [Manage access to projects, folders, and organizations](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/granting-changing-revoking-access#grant-single-role)."

title: Remove BETA tag from VETS tools
author: jonn-smith

title: ETA on new release built with htsjdk 4.3.0 for CRAM 3.1 support
author: EddieLF
body:
Are there any plans for a new release that includes htsjdk 4.3.0 for compatibility with CRAM 3.1?

Naively it seems as simple as bumping the version of htsjdk in the `build.gradle` file like [this commit](https://github.com/broadinstitute/gatk/commit/76edc75c26504da94bbaee66584e107e76ee15de) did for the last version increment.

It would be awesome to get support for CRAM 3.1 in a new GATK release instead of having to depend on the unstable nightly snapshots. Thanks

title: Funcotator - BRCA2 mutation with ambiguous annotation
author: BarrecaM
body:
A BRCA2 mutation is likely annotated ambiguously by Funcotator.

The BRCA2 mutation was identified in vcf as a deletion and insertion:
chr13	32936775	.	GAATTT	AG	1550.18	PASS

In the maf file annotated by Funcotator I was not able to find the same mutation, but another in the same mutational context:
BRCA2		hg19	chr13	32936776	32936780	Frame_Shift_Del	DEL		ATTT	-

Intriguingly, this mutation, defined in cDNA_Change column as c.7923_7926delATTT, was annotated by Funcotator with the ID NC_000013.10:g.32936775_32936780delinsAG for ClinVar_VCF_CLNHGVS and chr13:g.32936775_32936780=,chr13:g.32936775_32936780delinsAG for dbSNP_CLNHGVS, underlying that it is the same mutation found from the variant calling of the vcf. In the first moment, I thought they were synonymous mutations, but something didn't convince me.

Considering the reference context TTTCCTAAG **GAATTT** GCTAATAGAT, the two annotations give different mutations:
- c.7923_7926delATTT --> TTTCCTAAG **GA_** GCTAATAGAT
- NC_000013.10:g.32936775_32936780delinsAG --> TTTCCTAAG **_AG_** GCTAATAGAT

I think something's off in this case, but I could not tell you what the problem is. 
Could someone help me to understand whether the annotation is wrong or if I'm missing something?

title: Bitwise OR of a signed byte value
author: scuniff

title: UmiAwareMarkDuplicatesWithMateCigar does not honour --TAGGING_POLICY
author: DaGaMs
body:
Hi,

I'm trying to deduplicate a DNA-seq library that was created using Kapa UMI adapters. Deduplication works fine, but the resulting BAM file has not `DT:Z` tags despite `--TAGGING_POLICY All` being set:

```bash
java -jar gatk-package-4.6.2.0-local.jar UmiAwareMarkDuplicatesWithMateCigar 
    -I input.bam 
    -O output.bam 
    -M output_report.txt  
    --UMI_METRICS output_umi_metrics.txt 
    --TMP_DIR .  
    --CREATE_INDEX true 
    --ASO coordinate   
    -R hg38_full_gatk.fa.gz  
    --TAGGING_POLICY All  
    --OPTICAL_DUPLICATE_PIXEL_DISTANCE 4000 
    --VALIDATION_STRINGENCY LENIENT 
```

Counting DT:Z tags:

```bash
samtools view output.bam | fgrep -c DT:Z
0
```

Looking at the duplication metrics file, there should be plenty of duplicates:

```
LIBRARY UNPAIRED_READS_EXAMINED READ_PAIRS_EXAMINED     SECONDARY_OR_SUPPLEMENTARY_RDS  UNMAPPED_READS  UNPAIRED_READ_DUPLICATES        READ_PAIR_DUPLICATES    READ_PAIR_OPTICAL_DUPLICATES    PERCENT_DUPLICATION           ESTIMATED_LIBRARY_SIZE
S6      12658   70646386        6360591 101894  2733    15411529        10325302        0.21815 337291663
```

The same command line works fine with "normal" `MarkDuplicates`, so I suspect that the UMI version doesn't honour the `--TAGGING_POLICY` even though it is listed in the documentation.

title: issue with SelectVariants output file
author: hddesai
body: We use https://gatk.broadinstitute.org/hc/en-us/articles/360037055952-SelectVariants#:~:text=Arguments%20in%20this%20li to select for Indels from a VCF file. However, we recently noticed, that not all expected indels make it to the output indels list. Why is this the case? From what we've read, as long as at least one ALT allele is an indel, it should be in the parsed output. We don't apply any other filtering when selecting for indels. The command we use is gatk SelectVariants -V test.rg.MD.mutect2.filt.vcf.gz --select-type INDEL -O test.rg.MD.mutect2.filt.indels.vcf. This is observed for gatk 4.2.6 and 4.6.2.

title: GATK 4.6.2.0 includes unresolved Critical CVE (CVE-2024-51504)

Hi team,

We noticed that the recently released GATK 4.6.2.0 includes one Critical and fourteen High-severity CVEs.

Could you please clarify whether there is a plan or timeline to address the remaining vulnerabilities or at least the Critical issue?
[CVE-2024-51504](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-51504)

Thanks in advance for any guidance or updates.

title: Resource bundle access denied
author: sbaross

title: Use of java.util.Random to generate seed causes failure
author: MattWellie
body:
https://github.com/broadinstitute/gatk/blob/3cbea75161a7f334aba5c64681f9384db5b9970f/src/main/java/org/broadinstitute/hellbender/tools/copynumber/GermlineCNVCaller.java#L367-L384

Based on the documentation [here](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/util/Random.html) the use of `Random.nextInt()` returns any integer, so has a 50% chance of returning a negative value. Negative values are not acceptable arguments to the python script this process is preparing arguments for:

(from GATK-SV logs)
```
01:26:00.580 INFO  GermlineCNVCaller - The inference failed to converge and will be restarted once with a different random seed.
...
01:26:00.586 DEBUG ScriptExecutor -   /mnt/disks/cromwell_root/tmp.MOkT0J/case_denoising_calling.6807924686880120959.py
01:26:00.586 DEBUG ScriptExecutor -   --ploidy_calls_path=/mnt/disks/cromwell_root/contig-ploidy-calls-dir
01:26:00.586 DEBUG ScriptExecutor -   --output_calls_path=/mnt/disks/cromwell_root/out/case-calls
01:26:00.586 DEBUG ScriptExecutor -   --output_tracking_path=/mnt/disks/cromwell_root/out/case-tracking
01:26:00.586 DEBUG ScriptExecutor -   --input_model_path=/mnt/disks/cromwell_root/gcnv-model
01:26:00.586 DEBUG ScriptExecutor -   --random_seed=-1623339239
01:26:00.586 DEBUG ScriptExecutor -   --read_count_tsv_files
....

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/mnt/disks/cromwell_root/tmp.MOkT0J/case_denoising_calling.6807924686880120959.py", line 199, in <module>
    task = gcnvkernel.CaseDenoisingCallingTask(
  File "/opt/miniconda/envs/gatk/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gcnvkernel/tasks/task_case_denoising_calling.py", line 120, in __init__
    super().__init__(hybrid_inference_params, denoising_model, copy_number_emission_sampler, copy_number_caller,
  File "/opt/miniconda/envs/gatk/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gcnvkernel/tasks/inference_task_base.py", line 279, in __init__
    self.continuous_model_advi = ADVIDeterministicAnnealing(
  File "/opt/miniconda/envs/gatk/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gcnvkernel/inference/deterministic_annealing.py", line 47, in __init__
    approx = MeanField(local_rv=local_rv,
  File "/opt/miniconda/envs/gatk/lib/python3.10/site-packages/pymc/variational/approximations.py", line 338, in __init__
    groups = [self._group_class(None, *args, **kwargs)]
  File "/opt/miniconda/envs/gatk/lib/python3.10/site-packages/pymc/variational/opvi.py", line 746, in __init__
    self.rng = np.random.RandomState(random_seed)
  File "numpy/random/mtrand.pyx", line 185, in numpy.random.mtrand.RandomState.__init__
  File "_mt19937.pyx", line 168, in numpy.random._mt19937.MT19937._legacy_seeding
  File "_mt19937.pyx", line 182, in numpy.random._mt19937.MT19937._legacy_seeding
ValueError: Seed must be between 0 and 2**32 - 1
01:26:20.754 INFO  GermlineCNVCaller - Shutting down engine
[January 29, 2026 at 1:26:20 AM GMT] org.broadinstitute.hellbender.tools.copynumber.GermlineCNVCaller done. Elapsed time: 14.50 minutes.
Runtime.totalMemory()=5968494592
org.broadinstitute.hellbender.utils.python.PythonScriptExecutorException: 
python exited with 1
```

Swapping this out for `Random.nextInt(int Bound)` should give a guaranteed positive value. Or running an `abs(input)` on the input seed before it's passed through to the `gcnvkernel` module.

title: Funcotator cannot read integer value for start/end position
author: chstubbe
body:
GATK version: GATK/4.6.0.0

gatk command
gatk Funcotator \
    --variant $filtered_vcf_chr \
    --reference $reference_fasta \
    --ref-version hg19 \
    --data-sources-path $funcotator_data_sources \
    --output $output_maf \
    --output-file-format MAF \
    --transcript-list ${output_dir}/exclude_transcripts.txt \
    --annotation-default normal_barcode:none \
    --lenient true \
    --disable-sequence-dictionary-validation true \
    --verbosity DEBUG

functotator version: funcotator_dataSources.v1.8.hg19.20230908s"

I get this error: A USER ERROR has occurred: Unknown file is malformed: Cannot read integer value for start/end position!
I know my vcf is okay. The code runs until chr3 and then stops at a random position. Could someone help me?

Thanks in advance!

Kind regards
Charlotte

title: Issue with Calculating FS and SOR in GATK VariantAnnotator
author: Joyjoyjoyc
body:
Hello, I encountered an issue while using the following command:
```
gatk VariantAnnotator \
-R $REF \
-I $BAM \
-V $TMP_VCF \
-O $ANNOTATED_VCF \
-A BaseQualityRankSumTest \
-A ReadPosRankSumTest \
-A Coverage \
-A ExcessHet
```
I noticed that the INFO fields FS and SOR could not be calculated. I would like to know what information is required to compute these two fields, since my input VCF was generated by Strelka. Thank you so much!

title: CalculateContamination, OutOfRangeException, IllegalArgumentException: log10p: Log10-probability
author: feiloo
body:
Running CalculateContamination on a certain WGS pileup raises the Exceptions below.
The issue happens atleast with gatk 4.6.2 and gatk 4.4.0.0 from bioconda.
We might not be able to provide the input.table file. It is currently our only input file with this issue, others worked fine.
This issue appears when running on more than the first 5485300 rows of input.table. It doesnt seem to be a particular row.

command:
```
gatk CalculateContamination \
--java-options "-Djava.io.tmpdir=tmp -Xms50G -Xmx50G" \
--input input.table \
--verbosity DEBUG \
--output "output.table"
```

title: Mutect2 --pileup-detection true not working with a normal control?
author: Rmulet
body: Hi, I am using Mutect2 (GATK-4.6.2) to call variants in RNA-seq data that have been processed with SplitNCigar. As a result, when a variant is at the beginning or end of an exon, it is only supported by reads that start or end in that position. Apparently, this can be problematic for Mutect2, since some of these variants are not called. I attach an example below, where the first track corresponds to a tumor sample that carries a T>C mutation. When running Mutect2 with default settings, this mutation is completely missed. However, this can be recovered by setting `--pileup-detection` to true **in tumor-only mode**. These are the stats reported by Mutect2 for this variant: ``` chr12 112477651 . T C . . AS_SB_TABLE=29,21|2,4;DP=60;ECNT=1;ECNTH=1;MBQ=37,37;MFRL=4912,13285;MMQ=60,60;MPOS=45;POPAF=7.30;TLOD=16.13 GT:AD:AF:DP:F1R2:F2R1:FAD:SB 0/1:50,6:0.134:56:0,0:44,6:44,6:29,21,2,4 ``` Unfortunately, the mutation is missed again if I run the analysis with an unmatched normal control that has no reads supporting that mutation (second track in the image below). Perhaps pileup-based haplotypes are only being used in tumor-only mode? I have tried the suggestions in [When HaplotypeCaller and Mutect2 do not call an expected variant](https://gatk.broadinstitute.org/hc/en-us/articles/360043491652-When-HaplotypeCaller-and-Mutect2-do-not-call-an-expected-variant), but nothing seems to help. I have run Mutect2 with `--linked-de-bruijn-graph` , `--recover-all-dangling-branches` or `--min-pruning 0`, to no avail. This is what my command looked like after trying everything I could: `Mutect2 --reference /work/ALL/genome/GRCh38.primary_assembly.genome.fa --input 6371.bam --tumor-sample 6371 --output 6371_test_control.vcf --dont-use-soft-clipped-bases --native-pair-hmm-threads 5 --read-validation-stringency LENIENT --min-read-length 1 --intervals PTPN11.bed --initial-tumor-lod 1 --disable-tool-default-read-filters --lenient --recover-all-dangling-branches --min-pruning 0 --bam-output 6371_mutect.bam --input CONTROL.bam --normal-sample CONTROL` The BAM output of this command is in the last IGV track of my image. As you can see, it is completely empty. If I set `--pileup-detection` to false again and generate a GVCF, this is what it looks like: ``` chr12 112477651 . T . . END=112477651 GT:DP:MIN_DP:TLOD 0/0:59:59:15.58 ``` As a side note, I can only generate a GVCF without the normal, otherwise it gives me an error. UPDATE: The number of variants with and without `--pileup-detection` is different, so apparently it does work in tumor/normal mode. However, I find it strange that this the variant can be detected with this setting in tumor-only mode, but not when a normal is used (even though this normal sample has no reads supporting it). UPDATE2: after playing around with pretty much every setting of Mutect2, I have been able to detect this mutation by setting `--min-dangling-branch-length` to 1. But if dangling branches are indeed the issue with this variant, why doesn't `--recover-all-dangling-branches` work?
